title: ConvertKit vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: ConvertKit vs Drip: compare pricing, features, and real use cases to find the right email tool for your needs.
intro: Choosing between ConvertKit and Drip comes down to your specific use case. Both are legitimate email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. ConvertKit is built for creators, bloggers, and course sellers, while Drip targets e-commerce businesses wanting deep store integration. This comparison covers the key differences so you can decide quickly.

comparison_table.tool_b: E-Commerce Businesses Wanting Deep Store Integration

choose_a_if: You are primarily focused on creators, bloggers, and course sellers
choose_b_if: You are primarily focused on e-commerce businesses wanting deep store integration

faq.a: It depends on your use case. ConvertKit wins for creators, bloggers, and course sellers, while Drip is stronger for e-commerce businesses wanting deep store integration. Neither is objectively better — the right choice depends on your specific needs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or Drip?
faq.a: ConvertKit starts at a lower price point ($29/mo vs $39/mo). However, pricing scales with subscriber count, so the total cost depends on your list size.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, switching from Drip to ConvertKit is possible. Most platforms support CSV import/export for subscriber lists. Plan for some time to recreate automations and templates, as these rarely transfer directly between platforms.
